narrative.summary: Split-personality stunner — light & playful upstairs, dark & dramatic downstairs; Star Wine List 2026 pick. Worth a deliberate stop for John’s design/architecture/art/food radar.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on Queen West where it bleeds into Ossington, and you walk into a restaurant that can't decide what it wants to be—so it's both. Upstairs is all natural light and cream-colored upholstery, the kind of space where you'd order a picnic-style lunch. Downstairs flips the script entirely: dark, moody, dramatic lighting that makes every cocktail look like it's glowing from within.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: The menu splits its personality too—upstairs leans into a chef's picnic format, shareable and light, while the whole operation runs on a wine program serious enough to land a Star Wine List nod. The cocktails hit hard (their Old Fashioned apparently earns loyalty), and the kitchen knows how to pace a tasting menu that actually fills you up. It's the kind of place where the cutlery and plating matter as much as what's on the plate—every element is considered, nothing accidental.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Book upstairs for a birthday lunch where you want things to feel special without the pressure of a dark room and a $200 bill. Save downstairs for when you want wine-focused drama—a second date, maybe, or the kind of dinner where you're willing to linger over multiple bottles.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "We chose above floor experience with chef’s picnic menu - it was really good and rather filling. Cocktails were great too.

We had to wait longer for some orders because something happened with the order system, but they offered complimentary tea to make up for it, that was nice.

One of the bathrooms had broken door which locked me and I couldn’t open it for some time, I hope they fix it."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "I’m so glad I found pictures of this beautiful space while looking for somewhere special to take my Wife for her birthday. The decor, upholstery, drinks, plates and even cutlery was visual splendor, only outdone by the food itself!
Our server’s feedback from our questions about the menu helped guide us to a beyond-perfect experience.
My drink of choice is always an Old Fashioned and their signature version was an incredible elevation of a timeless classic. I don’t like mine too sweet, so replacing the simple syrup with fresh spun cotton candy as a garnish is absolutely genius. I couldn’t resist nibbling the majority of it before melting the rest into my glass. Even halfway through the drink I had to exclaim to my Wife how much I was enjoying the subtle flavour of the peach bitters and earl grey syrup.
Everything we ordered was incredible and would order again. I don’t think my vocabulary is deep enough to cover all the food, but I can say we were fan-girling over the chili present in the deviled eggs. We were sharing everything we ordered, but became slightly reluctant to when it was time for the pan seared salmon and roasted half chicken.
We finished with the toffee cake and coffee for desert which came with a candle and birthday wishes written in chocolate. I tried drizzling some coffee on my last bites of cake and was delighted by how well the flavour addition worked.
We got our coats and chatted with the staff briefly before leaving in a state of almost shock as we realized that might have been the best dinning experience we’ve ever had. We’re already looking forward to returning, and now have a new favourite spot in town. Long live AndOre!!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Such a unique experience to dine here.
The decor, the elegance, the lighting, the staff, the food — are all worth the hype around this magnificent restaurant. Our server was the most friendly and welcoming person we’ve interacted with in a while. Came here for my girlfriend’s birthday celebration, and he definitely made our night special. We didn’t dine in the cave, but he did give us a tour of the restaurant and the cave which was really nice. The food itself was amazing. Everything tasted was delicious and presented beautifully. Must try’s included: devilled egg, crispy potato pavé, smoked lamb belly, PORK SHOULDER WAS INCREDIBLE! Try as much as you can, you won’t regret it. Plates are portioned perfectly to share as well. The entire restaurant is absolutely beautiful to look at. The walls are hand painted throughout, giving you a feeling like you’re dining in heaven. For the under ground, the entire cave walls were hand chipped to give authenticity, and it really gives you the impression that you are deep underground. Something you need to just feel. This place is more than just a dinner spot, it’s an experience you will always remember and look back to. Shoutout to our amazing above ground server who I can’t remember his name but was absolute delight to dine with. We will definitely be back!

Ps, probably the coolest looking bar you’ll ever see!"
